mission.title: kyverno: [Feature] Allow Kyverno CLI output to file for easier CI/CD integration
mission.description: [Feature] Allow Kyverno CLI output to file for easier CI/CD integration. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
### Problem Statement

Kyverno CLI commands ```test``` and ```apply``` don't have an option to output their results to a separate, structured file.  Both always print to the terminal.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] Allow Kyverno CLI output to file for easier CI/CD
steps.description:
## Explanation
This PR enhances the test CLI command by introducing a new --output-format flag that supports multiple output formats, including ```json``` ```yaml``` ```markdown``` ```junit```.

## Related issue

## Milestone of this PR

## Proposed Changes
We can now use the --output-format flag
```yaml
$ kubectl-kyverno test . --output-format json

WARNING: test file (kyverno-test.yaml) uses a deprecated schema that will be removed in 1.14
Loading test  ( kyverno-test.yaml ) ...
  Loading values/variables ...
  Loading policies ...
  Loading resources ...
  Loading exceptions ...
  Applying 2 policies to 4 resources with 0 exceptions ...
  Checking results ...

[
  {
    "ID": 1,
    "POLICY": "require-requests-limits",
    "REASON": "Want pass, got fail",
    "RESOURCE": "v1/Pod/default/badpod01",
    "RESULT": "Fail",
    "RULE": "validate-resources"
  },
  {
    "ID": 2,
    "POLICY": "re
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] Allow Kyverno CLI output to file for easier CI/CD" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Explanation
This PR enhances the test CLI command by introducing a new --output-format flag that supports multiple output formats, including ```json``` ```yaml``` ```markdown``` ```junit```.

## Related issue

## Milestone of this PR

## Proposed Changes
We can now use the --output-format flag with an input string out of available formats ```json``` ```yaml``` ```markdown``` ```junit```.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
